title: cnidoscolus urens var. neglectus
intro: Cnidoscolus urens var. neglectus, also known as the cactus pear or barbados gooseberry, is a type of spiny succulent plant native to the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can help to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's sap can be dried and used to make a powder, or the leaves can be steeped in water to make a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
